abstract_ts: Nowadays, the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) models with digital humans presents a novel approach to enhancing English oral teaching. This study explores the application of digital humans within the framework of man-machine collaborative teaching systems, focusing on the potential to improve interaction, engagement, and personalized learning in English oral instruction and micro-course teaching. By analyzing current implementations and pedagogical outcomes, this research identifies the necessities, challenges, and strategies for incorporating AI-driven digital humans into language teaching practices. We present evidence-based insights into how digital humans can support English language learners’ and educators' dynamic needs. We've developed and tested an English teaching system using digital human technology as its core. The practice suggests that digital humans, powered by sophisticated AI models, offer a unique opportunity to enrich the oral language learning experience, thereby paving the way for innovative teaching methodologies in the digital age.

abstract_ts: An androgen-dependent mouse mammary carcinoma cell line (SC-3) requires androgen for growth stimulation. We have shown previously that androgen acts on SC-3 cells to induce secretion of a fibroblast growth factor (FGF)-like growth factor, which in turn stimulates growth of the cells in an autocrine manner. In this study, the androgen-induced growth factor (AIGF) was purified from a conditioned medium of SC-3 cells stimulated with testosterone. cDNA cloning of AIGF by use of its partial amino acid sequence data revealed that AIGF is a distinctive FGF-like growth factor. An AIGF cDNA (pSC17) encodes a 215-amino acid protein with a putative signal peptide, which shares 30-40% homology with known members of the FGF family. The AIGF mRNA was markedly induced by 10 nM testosterone in Northern blot analysis. Expression of AIGF cDNA in mammalian cells clearly showed remarkable stimulatory effects of AIGF on growth of SC-3 cells in the absence of androgen. Thus, it is clear that the androgen-induced growth of SC-3 cells is mediated in an autocrine manner by AIGF, which is secreted by the tumor cells themselves in response to hormonal stimuli.

abstract_ts: This chapter gives a striking account of the zoonotic potential of Cryptosporidium. Cryptosporidiosis is an infectious disease of animal origin, but the role of animals in the epidemiology of human cryptosporidial infections remains vague. Re-emerging zoonotic diseases are of major concern since of the perils in controlling infections with an unidentified assemblage of animal reservoirs and the difficulties of evaluating the public health risk of a spillover of disease transmission to humans. Any determination of zoonotic potential is not only purely dependent upon evidence that an infectious agent is shared in nature by humans and other vertebrates, but also on evidence of transmission between the two. In this respect, there is abundant evidence of Cryptosporidium's zoonotic potential. Cryptosporidium parvum or C. parvum-like organisms have been reported from 152 species of mammals, but it is information on the frequency of zoonotic transmission that is lacking. The association of human infection with exposure to infected livestock, particularly young cattle or sheep, has been reported previously..

abstract_ts: This chapter illustrates the role of flowers in Paduan culture, politics, and the economy. During the first half of the nineteenth century, the interest in plants of many prominent figures in the city’s social and intellectual elite was more than a temporary fashion or a social convention, but an expression of a long-standing tradition. A focus on the floral exhibitions of the late 1840s highlights a love for plants, but also several significant socio-political features and trends of the time. Even more than the Gardening Society, the Festival of Flowers transgressed regional, social, and gender boundaries. It saw the active participation of many women and of plant lovers from other provinces and regions. The attendance of many Austrian officials together with their wives suggests a close relationship between Veneto’s social elite and the subjects of ‘flower emperor’ Francis I. On the other hand, the growing number of participants from the lower classes reveals that important social and economic changes were underway. Flowers ceased to be objects by means of which the nobility set itself apart. In addition, the increasing attraction of flowers created new markets, which were readily supplied by a growing number of floricultural industries.

abstract_ts: A new gallophosphate was synthesized in aqueous medium using the macrocycle 1,4,8,11-tetraazacyclotetradecane as organic species. The structure was determined by single crystal X-ray diffraction. The symmetry is monoclinic, space group C2/c, a=0.8842(3)nm, b=1.7781(2)nm, c=1.5805(3)nm, β= 92.05(3)°. The structure consists of gallophosphate chains of gallium-corner-sharing Ga2P2O4 four-rings. Each chain is connected to the others via a gallium-organic complex leading to a three dimensional framework. This new gallophosphate shows low thermal stability, a complete amorphization being observed after calcination at 350°C. The results of 13C and 31P solid state nmr spectroscopy are in agreement with the structure analysis.
